generatedSentence: Amidst much backlogs, the doughty, resilient clerk felt whelmed in the mart's vicinity. Lowly workers churned tacky kraft paper, their autos glued onsite near old lamps. He imagined himself a lifeguard for the wanderers on these roads, finding comfort in a fading mythos, not just a dormouse chasing a meager gain. But crime highjacks often cut into the system, creating new backlogs. An evicted digger, kinged by desperation, wielded a pistol with a flashy lollipop, demanding jaggery. A dancer, fidgety and rocking, distracted the guard, while the tickly glint of plutonium reflected in the bar’s window, signifying an approaching rollercoaster of trouble. The researcher, observing the whiteout from his fort, knew it would happen. His grimoire, filled with lyrics and mythos, hinted at the source. Carpenters onsite had once found nuggets of evidence, now a flickering memory, that an ungulate creature, much like a dormouse, emitted hypersonic frequencies from the melting core of the facility, causing the disruption. Vindicated, the doughty team sprang into emergency action. They knew unsticking the firewalls would require a stellar effort to emit a counter-frequency. The ranger, with a frankincense leaflet, gave his okays to go along. They felt the everlasting comfort of victory as the system returned, a comedy of errors resolved, the ulna of their struggle now a memory.
panelSentences: Amidst much backlogs, the doughty, resilient clerk felt whelmed in the mart's vicinity. Lowly workers churned tacky kraft paper, their autos glued onsite near old lamps. He imagined himself a lifeguard for the wanderers on these roads, finding comfort in a fading mythos, not just a dormouse chasing a meager gain.

But crime highjacks often cut into the system, creating new backlogs. An evicted digger, kinged by desperation, wielded a pistol with a flashy lollipop, demanding jaggery. A dancer, fidgety and rocking, distracted the guard, while the tickly glint of plutonium reflected in the bar’s window, signifying an approaching rollercoaster of trouble.

The researcher, observing the whiteout from his fort, knew it would happen. His grimoire, filled with lyrics and mythos, hinted at the source. Carpenters onsite had once found nuggets of evidence, now a flickering memory, that an ungulate creature, much like a dormouse, emitted hypersonic frequencies from the melting core of the facility, causing the disruption.

Vindicated, the doughty team sprang into emergency action. They knew unsticking the firewalls would require a stellar effort to emit a counter-frequency. The ranger, with a frankincense leaflet, gave his okays to go along. They felt the everlasting comfort of victory as the system returned, a comedy of errors resolved, the ulna of their struggle now a memory.
rawPanelSentences: Amidst much backlogs, the doughty, resilient clerk felt whelmed in the mart's vicinity. Lowly workers churned tacky kraft paper, their autos glued onsite near old lamps. He imagined himself a lifeguard for the wanderers on these roads, finding comfort in a fading mythos, not just a dormouse chasing a meager gain.

But crime highjacks often cut into the system, creating new backlogs. An evicted digger, kinged by desperation, wielded a pistol with a flashy lollipop, demanding jaggery. A dancer, fidgety and rocking, distracted the guard, while the tickly glint of plutonium reflected in the bar’s window, signifying an approaching rollercoaster of trouble.

The researcher, observing the whiteout from his fort, knew it would happen. His grimoire, filled with lyrics and mythos, hinted at the source. Carpenters onsite had once found nuggets of evidence, now a flickering memory, that an ungulate creature, much like a dormouse, emitted hypersonic frequencies from the melting core of the facility, causing the disruption.

Vindicated, the doughty team sprang into emergency action. They knew unsticking the firewalls would require a stellar effort to emit a counter-frequency. The ranger, with a frankincense leaflet, gave his okays to go along. They felt the everlasting comfort of victory as the system returned, a comedy of errors resolved, the ulna of their struggle now a memory.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined female technician with braided silver hair and glowing blue ocular implants. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, seen from a medium shot, hunched over a holographic interface, frustrated by a cascade of glowing red error messages. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist in a wide shot, leaping over an overturned, smoking auto, a shadowy figure with a pistol visible in the background. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a close-up, eyes wide with discovery, holding a glowing data shard that projects complex symbols. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, victorious and resolute, standing atop a restored, brightly lit server core, one hand on her hip, looking towards the viewer from a low angle.

definitions.definition: A person who is bold or brave.
Bold; brave, courageous.
An item of confectionery consisting of a piece of candy/sweet attached to a stick.
Endorsement; approval.

definitions.definition: To approve.
definitions.example: I don't want to OK this amount of money.

definitions.definition: To confirm by activating a button marked OK.
(of objects or substances) Returning quickly to original shape after force is applied; elastic.
(organisms or people, of systems) Returning quickly to normal after damaging events or conditions.

A way used for travelling between places, originally one wide enough to allow foot passengers and horses to travel, now (US) usually one surfaced with asphalt or concrete and designed to accommodate many vehicles travelling in both directions. In the UK both senses are heard: a country road is the same as a country lane.
A road; or particularly a car, as a means of transportation.
definitions.example: We travelled to the seaside by road.

definitions.definition: A path chosen in life or career.
An underground tunnel in a mine.
A railway or a single railway track.
The act of riding on horseback.
A hostile ride against a particular area; a raid.
(often in the plural) A partly sheltered area of water near a shore in which vessels may ride at anchor; a roadstead.
A journey, or stage of a journey.
A way or route.
(occasionally in the singular) A roadstead.
A fireproof barrier used to prevent the spread of fire between or through buildings, structures, electrical substation transformers, or within an aircraft or vehicle.
The software that monitors traffic in and out of a private network or a personal computer and allows or blocks such traffic depending on its perceived threat.
A hypothetical phenomenon where an observer falling into a black hole encounters high-energy quanta at or near the event horizon.
Showy; visually impressive, attention-getting, or appealing.
definitions.example: The dancers wore flashy costumes featuring shiny sequins in many vibrant colors.

definitions.definition: Flashing; producing flashes.
definitions.example: a flashy light

definitions.definition: Drunk; tipsy
A traditional unrefined sugar used throughout South and South-East Asia.
A small-scale production plant that processes sugar cane.
The act of cutting.
definitions.example: He made a fine cut with his sword.

definitions.definition: The result of cutting.
definitions.example: a smooth or clear cut

definitions.definition: An opening resulting from cutting; an incision or wound.
definitions.example: Look at this cut on my finger!

definitions.definition: A notch, passage, or channel made by cutting or digging; a furrow; a groove.
definitions.example: a cut for a railroad

definitions.definition: A share or portion.
definitions.example: The lawyer took a cut of the profits.

definitions.definition: A batsman's shot played with a swinging motion of the bat, to hit the ball backward of point.
Sideways movement of the ball through the air caused by a fast bowler imparting spin to the ball.
In lawn tennis, etc., a slanting stroke causing the ball to spin and bound irregularly; also, the spin thus given to the ball.

In a strokeplay competition, the early elimination of those players who have not then attained a preannounced score, so that the rest of the competition is less pressed for time and more entertaining for spectators.
A passage omitted or to be omitted from a play.
definitions.example: The director asked the cast to note down the following cuts.

definitions.definition: A particular version or edit of a film.
The act or right of dividing a deck of playing cards.
definitions.example: The player next to the dealer makes a cut by placing the bottom half on top.

definitions.definition: The card obtained by dividing the pack.
The manner or style a garment etc. is fashioned in.
definitions.example: I like the cut of that suit.

definitions.definition: A slab, especially of meat.
definitions.example: That’s our finest cut of meat.

definitions.definition: An attack made with a chopping motion of the blade, landing with its edge or point.
A deliberate snub, typically a refusal to return a bow or other acknowledgement of acquaintance.
An unkind act; a cruelty.
A definable part, such as an individual song, of a recording, particularly of commercial records, audio tapes, CDs, etc.
definitions.example: The drummer on the last cut of their CD is not identified.

definitions.definition: A truncation, a context that represents a moment in time when other archaeological deposits were removed for the creation of some feature such as a ditch or pit.
A haircut.
The partition of a graph’s vertices into two subgroups.
A string of railway cars coupled together.
An engraved block or plate; the impression from such an engraving.
definitions.example: a book illustrated with fine cuts

definitions.definition: A common workhorse; a gelding.
The failure of a college officer or student to be present at any appointed exercise.
A skein of yarn.
That which is used to dilute or adulterate a recreational drug.
definitions.example: Don't buy his coke: it's full of cut.

definitions.definition: A notch shaved into an eyebrow.
A time period when one tries to lose fat while retaining muscle mass.
A hidden or secure place.
(heading) To incise, to cut into the surface of something.
To admit of incision or severance; to yield to a cutting instrument.
(heading, social) To separate, remove, reject or reduce.
(audio, usually as imperative) To cease recording activities.
definitions.example: After the actors read their lines, the director yelled, "Cut!"

definitions.definition: To make an abrupt transition from one scene or image to another.
definitions.example: The camera then cut to the woman on the front row who was clearly overcome and crying tears of joy.

definitions.definition: To edit a film by selecting takes from original footage.
To remove and place in memory for later use.
definitions.example: Select the text, cut it, and then paste it in the other application.

definitions.definition: To enter a queue in the wrong place.
definitions.example: One student kept trying to cut in front of the line.

definitions.definition: To intersect or cross in such a way as to divide in half or nearly so.
definitions.example: This road cuts right through downtown.

definitions.definition: To make the ball spin sideways by running one's fingers down the side of the ball while bowling it.
To deflect (a bowled ball) to the off, with a chopping movement of the bat.
To change direction suddenly.

definitions.definition: To divide a pack of playing cards into two.

definitions.definition: To write.
definitions.example: cut orders;  cut a check

definitions.definition: To dilute or adulterate something, especially a recreational drug.

definitions.definition: To exhibit (a quality).
To stop or disengage.

definitions.definition: To drive (a ball) to one side, as by (in billiards or croquet) hitting it fine with another ball, or (in tennis) striking it with the racket inclined.
To lose body mass after bulking, aiming to keep the additional muscle but lose the fat.
To perform (a dancing movement etc.).
(participial adjective) Having been cut.
Reduced.

definitions.definition: Omitted from a literary, musical or other work.

definitions.definition: (of a gem) Carved into a shape; not raw.
(of a shot) Played with a horizontal bat to hit the ball backward of point.

Emotionally hurt.

An informal nickname for a friend; used as a term of endearment.

To crown king, to make (a person) king.

To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.

A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.

To hit, clout, belt, wallop.

A woodwind instrument consisting of a tube with a row of holes that produce sound through vibrations caused by air blown across the edge of the holes, often tuned by plugging one or more holes with a finger; the Western concert flute, a transverse side-blown flute of European origin.
A recorder, also a woodwind instrument.
A glass with a long, narrow bowl and a long stem, used for drinking wine, especially champagne.

A lengthwise groove, such as one of the lengthwise grooves on a classical column, or a groove on a cutting tool (such as a drill bit, endmill, or reamer), which helps to form both a cutting edge and a channel through which chips can escape
A semicylindrical vertical groove, as in a pillar, in plaited cloth, or in a rifle barrel to cut down the weight.

In the Church of England, the layman that assists in the church service, especially in reading the responses (also called parish clerk).

definitions.example: ... users are often unable to note down the number of the taxi or the auto as the driver will just zoom past.

definitions.definition: Of a substance, slightly sticky.

definitions.definition: A solid, more or less rigid object of metal or wood with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length.

definitions.definition: A solid metal object with uniform (round, square, hexagonal, octagonal or rectangular) cross-section; in the US its smallest dimension is 1/4 inch or greater, a piece of thinner material being called a strip.
definitions.example: Ancient Sparta used iron bars instead of handy coins in more valuable alloy, to physically discourage the use of money.

definitions.definition: A cuboid piece of any solid commodity.

definitions.definition: A broad shaft, or band, or stripe.

definitions.definition: A long, narrow drawn or printed rectangle, cuboid or cylinder, especially as used in a bar code or a bar chart.

A business licensed to sell alcoholic drinks for consumption on the premises, or the premises themselves; public house.

definitions.definition: The counter of such premises.

definitions.definition: A counter, or simply a cabinet, from which alcoholic drinks are served in a private house or a hotel room.
(by extension, in combinations such as coffee bar, juice bar etc.) Premises or a counter serving any type of beverage.
An establishment where alcohol and sometimes other refreshments are served.
An informal establishment selling food to be consumed on the premises.
definitions.example: a burger bar

definitions.definition: An establishment offering cosmetic services.
definitions.example: a nail bar; a brow bar

definitions.definition: An official order or pronouncement that prohibits some activity.
definitions.example: The club has lifted its bar on women members.

definitions.definition: Anything that obstructs, hinders, or prevents; an obstruction; a barrier.
(whimsical, derived from fubar) A metasyntactic variable representing an unspecified entity, often the second in a series, following foo.
definitions.example: Suppose we have two objects, foo and bar.

definitions.definition: (Parliament) A dividing line (physical or notional) in the chamber of a legislature beyond which only members and officials may pass.
The railing surrounding the part of a courtroom in which the judges, lawyers, defendants and witnesses stay
"the Bar" or "the bar" The bar exam, the legal licensing exam.
definitions.example: He's studying hard to pass the Bar this time; he's failed it twice before.

definitions.definition: (metonym, "the Bar", "the bar") Collectively, lawyers or the legal profession; specifically applied to barristers in some countries but including all lawyers in others.
definitions.example: He was called to the bar, he became a barrister.

definitions.definition: One of an array of bar-shaped symbols that display the level of something, such as wireless signal strength or battery life remaining.
definitions.example: I don't have any bars in the middle of this desert.

definitions.definition: A vertical line across a musical staff dividing written music into sections, typically of equal durational value.
One of those musical sections.
A horizontal pole that must be crossed in high jump and pole vault
Any level of achievement regarded as a challenge to be overcome.
(most codes) The crossbar.
The central divider between the inner and outer table of a backgammon board, where stones are placed if they are hit.
An addition to a military medal, on account of a subsequent act
A linear shoaling landform feature within a body of water.
A ridge or succession of ridges of sand or other substance, especially a formation extending across the mouth of a river or harbor or off a beach, and which may obstruct navigation. (FM 55-501).
One of the ordinaries in heraldry; a fess.
A city gate, in some British place names.
definitions.example: Potter's Bar

definitions.definition: A drilling or tamping rod.
A vein or dike crossing a lode.
A gatehouse of a castle or fortified town.
The part of the crust of a horse's hoof which is bent inwards towards the frog at the heel on each side, and extends into the centre of the sole.
(in the plural) The space between the tusks and grinders in the upper jaw of a horse, in which the bit is placed.
Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.
definitions.example: There is no evidence that anyone was here earlier.

definitions.definition: Anything admitted by a court to prove or disprove alleged matters of fact in a trial.
One who bears witness.
A body of objectively verifiable facts that are positively indicative of, and/or exclusively concordant with, that one conclusion over any other.
To provide evidence for, or suggest the truth of.
definitions.example: She was furious, as evidenced by her slamming the door.

definitions.definition: A short, quick movement, especially a brush, sweep, or flip.
definitions.example: He removed the speck of dust with a flick of his finger.

definitions.definition: A motion picture; (in plural, usually preceded by "the") movie theater, cinema.
definitions.example: My all-time favorite flick is "Gone with the Wind."

definitions.definition: A cut that lands with the point, often involving a whip of the foible of the blade to strike at a concealed target.
A powerful underarm volley shot.
The act of pressing a place on a touch screen device.
A flitch.
definitions.example: a flick of bacon

definitions.definition: A unit of time, equal to 1/705,600,000 of a second
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: A specific act committed in violation of the law.
Any great sin or wickedness; iniquity.
That which occasions crime.
Criminal acts collectively.
The habit or practice of committing crimes.
definitions.example: Crime doesn’t pay.

definitions.definition: To subject to disciplinary punishment.
To commit crime(s).

To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel. Or to drill, or the like, through rocks, roads, or the like. More generally, to make any similar hole by moving material out of the way.
definitions.example: If the plane can't pull out of the dive it is in, it'll dig a hole in the ground.

definitions.definition: To get by digging; to take from the ground; often with up.
definitions.example: to dig potatoes

definitions.definition: To take ore from its bed, in distinction from making excavations in search of ore.
To work like a digger; to study ploddingly and laboriously.
To investigate, to research, often followed by out or up.
definitions.example: to dig out the facts

definitions.definition: To thrust; to poke.
definitions.example: He dug an elbow into my ribs and guffawed at his own joke.

definitions.definition: To defend against an attack hit by the opposing team by successfully passing the ball
To understand or show interest in.
definitions.example: You dig?

definitions.definition: To appreciate, or like.
definitions.example: Baby, I dig you.

definitions.definition: The action performed by a person or thing that digs.
A place where ore is dug, especially certain localities in California, Australia, etc. where gold is obtained.
Region; locality
Short private road that leads to a house or garage.
A person who dances, usually as a job or profession.
A stripper.
The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.
The contents of such containers.
A specific bag or container holding a traveller's belongings.
A chance for advancement, progress or profit.
definitions.example: The world is full of opportunities and it's up to me to see them and pursue them.

definitions.definition: A favorable circumstance or occasion.
definitions.example: Having a holiday is a great opportunity to relax.

definitions.definition: (Euro-English) opportuneness
definitions.example: The Court questioned the opportunity of introducing these measures in such an uncertain economic climate.

definitions.definition: One who researches.
To hunt rabbits.
To flee.
definitions.example: The informant seemed skittish, as if he was about to rabbit.

definitions.definition: To talk incessantly and in a childish manner; to babble annoyingly.
definitions.example: Stop your infernal rabbiting! Use proper words or nobody will listen to you!

definitions.definition: (sometimes figurative) To free from the condition of being stuck.
Having, or pertaining to, a tendency to fidget; restless.
A small, compact chunk or clump.
definitions.example: a gold nugget

definitions.definition: A chicken nugget.
A tidbit of something valuable.
definitions.example: a nugget of wisdom

definitions.definition: A small piece of tasty food, a tidbit.
A type of boot polish.
A bud from the Cannabis sativa plant, especially one that is potent.
An inexperienced, newly trained fighter pilot.
A partial description gleaned from data mining.
The person, place or thing from which something (information, goods, etc.) comes or is acquired.
definitions.example: The accused refused to reveal the source of the illegal drugs she was selling.

definitions.definition: Spring; fountainhead; wellhead; any collection of water on or under the surface of the ground in which a stream originates.
definitions.example: The main sources of the Euphrates River are the Karasu and Murat Rivers.

definitions.definition: A reporter's informant.
Source code.
The name of one terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
To obtain or procure: used especially of a business resource.
To find information about (a quotation)'s source (from which it comes): to find a citation for.
To fuse or melt two things into one, especially in order to extract metal from ore; to meld
The process of melting or fusion, especially to extract a metal from its ore.
A person skilled at carpentry, the trade of cutting and joining timber in order to construct buildings or other structures.

A senior rating in ships responsible for all the woodwork onboard; in the days of sail, a warrant officer responsible for the hull, masts, spars and boats of a ship, and whose responsibility was to sound the well to see if the ship was making water.
A two-wheeled carriage.
A carpenter bee.
A woodlouse.
Contentment, ease.
definitions.example: Sleep in comfort with our new mattress.

definitions.definition: Something that offers comfort.
definitions.example: the comforts of home

definitions.definition: A consolation; something relieving suffering or worry.
definitions.example: We still have the spare tire? That's a comfort at least.

definitions.definition: A cause of relief or satisfaction.
definitions.example: The outcome of the peace negotiations in Moscow in 1940 was a heavy blow to the young nation, but in the same time a great comfort: at least the independency was preserved.

definitions.definition: To relieve the distress or suffering of; to provide comfort to.
definitions.example: Rob comforted Aaron because he was lost and very sad.

definitions.definition: To make comfortable.
To make strong; to invigorate; to fortify; to corroborate.
To assist or help; to aid.
To join or attach something using glue.
definitions.example: I need to glue the chair-leg back into place.

definitions.definition: To cause something to adhere closely to; to follow attentively.
definitions.example: His eyes were glued to the screen.

definitions.definition: On or at a site.
On or at a site.
A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.
The score for such a work.
A building designed for the performance of such works; an opera house.
A company dedicated to performing such works.
(by extension) Any showy, melodramatic or unrealistic production resembling an opera.
A sheet material used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.
A newspaper or anything used as such (such as a newsletter or listing magazine).
Wallpaper.
Wrapping paper.
(rock paper scissors) An open hand (a handshape resembling a sheet of paper), that beats rock and loses to scissors. It loses to lizard and beats Spock in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
A written document, generally shorter than a book (white paper, term paper), in particular one written for the Government.

A written document that reports scientific or academic research and is usually subjected to peer review before publication in a scientific journal (as a journal article or the manuscript for one) or in the proceedings of a scientific or academic meeting (such as a conference, workshop, or symposium).
A scholastic essay.
A set of examination questions to be answered at one session.
Money.
A university course.
A paper packet containing a quantity of items.
definitions.example: a paper of pins, tacks, opium, etc.

definitions.definition: A medicinal preparation spread upon paper, intended for external application.
definitions.example: cantharides paper

definitions.definition: A substance resembling paper secreted by certain invertebrates as protection for their nests and eggs.
Free passes of admission to a theatre, etc.
(by extension) The people admitted by free passes.
To apply paper to.
definitions.example: to paper the hallway walls

definitions.definition: To document; to memorialize.
definitions.example: After they reached an agreement, their staffs papered it up.

definitions.definition: To fill (a theatre or other paid event) with complimentary seats.
definitions.example: As the event has not sold well, we'll need to paper the house.

definitions.definition: To submit official papers to (a law court, etc.).
To give public notice (typically by displaying posters) that a person is wanted by the police or other authority.
To sandpaper.
To enfold in paper.
To paste the endpapers and flyleaves at the beginning and end of a book before fitting it into its covers.
Made of paper.
definitions.example: paper bag; paper plane

definitions.definition: Insubstantial (from the weakness of common paper)
definitions.example: paper tiger; paper gangster

definitions.definition: Planned (from plans being drawn up on paper)
definitions.example: paper rocket; paper engine

definitions.definition: Having a title that is merely official, or given by courtesy or convention.
definitions.example: a paper baron; a paper lord

definitions.definition: To clear of an accusation, suspicion or criticism.
definitions.example: to vindicate someone's honor

definitions.definition: To justify by providing evidence.
definitions.example: to vindicate a right, claim or title

definitions.definition: To maintain or defend (a cause) against opposition.
definitions.example: to vindicate the rights of labor movement in developing countries

definitions.definition: To provide justification for.
definitions.example: The violent history of the suspect vindicated the use of force by the police.

definitions.definition: To lay claim to; to assert a right to; to claim.
To liberate; to set free; to deliver.
To avenge; to punish
definitions.example: a war to vindicate infidelity

definitions.definition: Justified, avenged or cleared of blame
The highest or uppermost side or portion of something
A favourable aspect of something that also has an unfavourable aspect
An upward tendency, especially in a financial market etc
On the top of.
To draw or let out wholly; to drain off completely
definitions.example: Moisture of the earth is exhausted by evaporation.

definitions.definition: To empty by drawing or letting out the contents
definitions.example: to exhaust a treasury

definitions.definition: To drain; to use up or expend wholly, or until the supply comes to an end
definitions.example: I exhausted my strength walking up the hill.

definitions.definition: To tire out; to wear out; to cause to be without any energy
definitions.example: The marathon exhausted me.

definitions.definition: To bring out or develop completely
To discuss thoroughly or completely
definitions.example: That subject has already been fully exhausted.

definitions.definition: To subject to the action of various solvents in order to remove all soluble substances or extractives
definitions.example: to exhaust a drug successively with water, alcohol, and ether

definitions.definition: Very tired; in a state of exhaustion.
definitions.example: The exhausted man fell asleep immediately.

definitions.definition: Depleted of resources.
definitions.example: The exhausted mine was worthless once all the ore had been extracted.

definitions.definition: A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
A place used by another mammal, fish, amphibian or insect, for depositing eggs and hatching young.
A snug, comfortable, or cosy residence or job situation.
A retreat, or place of habitual resort.
A hideout for bad people to frequent or haunt; a den.
definitions.example: That nightclub is a nest of strange people!

definitions.definition: A home that a child or young adult shares with a parent or guardian.
definitions.example: I am aspiring to leave the nest.

definitions.definition: A fixed number of cards in some bidding games awarded to the highest bidder allowing him to exchange any or all with cards in his hand.
definitions.example: I was forced to change trumps when I found the ace, jack, and nine of diamonds in the nest.

definitions.definition: A fortified position for a weapon.
A structure consisting of nested structures, such as nested loops or nested subroutine calls.
A circular bed of pasta, rice, etc. to be topped or filled with other foods.
An aggregated mass of any ore or mineral, in an isolated state, within a rock.
A collection of boxes, cases, or the like, of graduated size, each put within the one next larger.
A compact group of pulleys, gears, springs, etc., working together or collectively.
(of animals) To build or settle into a nest.
To settle into a home.
definitions.example: We loved the new house and were nesting there in two days!

definitions.definition: To successively neatly fit inside another.
definitions.example: I bought a set of nesting mixing bowls for my mother.

definitions.definition: To place in, or as if in, a nest.
To place one thing neatly inside another, and both inside yet another (and so on).
definitions.example: There would be much more room in the attic if you had nested all the empty boxes.

definitions.definition: To hunt for birds' nests or their contents (usually "go nesting").
Sodium bicarbonate (usually baking soda).
Sodium carbonate (usually washing soda).
Sodium in chemical combination.
Carbonated water (water impregnated with pressurised carbon dioxide, originally made with sodium bicarbonate).
(especially in the northeast) Any carbonated (usually sweet) soft drink.
(especially in the northeast) A glass, bottle or can of this drink.
The first card in the dealing box in the game of faro, which is discarded to leave 51 cards in play.
A choric song of celebration or revel, especially in Ancient Greece.
A light, amusing play with a happy ending.
(Medieval Europe) A narrative poem with an agreeable ending (e.g., The Divine Comedy).
A dramatic work that is light and humorous or satirical in tone.
The genre of such works.
Entertainment composed of jokes, satire, or humorous performance.
definitions.example: Why would you be watching comedy when there are kids starving right now?

definitions.definition: The art of composing comedy.
A humorous event.
The act of chewing; mastication with the mouth.
definitions.example: I popped the gum into my mouth and gave it a chew.

definitions.definition: Level of chewiness.
A small sweet, such as a taffy, that is eaten by chewing.
definitions.example: Phillip purchased a bag of licorice chews at the drugstore.

definitions.definition: Chewing tobacco.
definitions.example: The school had banned chew and smokes from the school grounds, even for adults.

definitions.definition: A plug or wad of chewing tobacco; chaw or a chaw.
definitions.example: The ballplayers sat on the bench watching the rain, glumly working their chews.

definitions.definition: The condition of something being torn or ground up mechanically.
To crush with the teeth by repeated closing and opening of the jaws; done to food to soften it and break it down by the action of saliva before it is swallowed.
definitions.example: Make sure to chew thoroughly, and don't talk with your mouth full!

definitions.definition: To grind, tear, or otherwise degrade or demolish something with teeth or as with teeth.
definitions.example: He keeps his feed in steel drums to prevent the mice from chewing holes in the feed-sacks.

definitions.definition: To think about something; to ponder; to chew over.
definitions.example: The professor stood at the blackboard, chalk in hand, and chewed the question the student had asked.

definitions.definition: A book of instructions in the use of alchemy or magic, especially one containing spells for summoning demons.
To halt one's work or other activity.
definitions.example: I think I'll knock off for the evening and go to bed.

definitions.definition: To kill.
definitions.example: The mobsters hired the guy to knock off their enemies.

definitions.definition: To remove, as a discount or estimate.
definitions.example: They agreed to knock off 20% of the price.

definitions.definition: To rob.
definitions.example: They decided to knock off a liquor store downtown.

definitions.definition: To make a copy of, as of a design.
definitions.example: They send people to the shows in Milan for "ideas", which means knocking off the designs they guess would sell.

definitions.definition: To assign (an item) to a bidder at an auction, indicated by knocking on the counter.
To have sex with.
To accomplish hastily.
definitions.example: I knocked off a couple of quick sketches before the design meeting.

definitions.definition: An imitation of something, particularly a well-known product, usually lower in quality and price than the original.
definitions.example: It's not a name-brand bag; it's just a cheap knockoff.

definitions.definition: A device in a knitting machine to remove loops from the needles.
Of or pertaining to an entrance or threshold.
Of or pertaining to a beginning or first stage of a process.
A sanctuary dedicated to the Ancient Greek and Roman god Pluto, usually at a location producing poisonous emissions, believed to represent an entrance to the underworld.
The transuranic chemical element with atomic number 94 and symbol Pu: a silvery-gray radioactive actinide metal that tarnishes when exposed to air.
To move backwards, especially for a vehicle to do so.
definitions.example: That beeping sound indicates that the truck is backing up.

definitions.definition: To move a vehicle backwards.
definitions.example: Back up the car a little, you're blocking the driveway.

definitions.definition: To undo one's actions.
definitions.example: I couldn't see how to finish the project, so I backed up and tried it another way.

definitions.definition: To reconsider one's thoughts.
definitions.example: This isn't working. Let's back up and think about it.

definitions.definition: To copy (data) so that it can be restored if the main copy is lost.
definitions.example: Back up your documents folder before applying the update.

definitions.definition: To provide support or the promise of support to.
definitions.example: When he said I wasn't there, I told him I was, and my buddy backed me up.

definitions.definition: For the non-striker to take a few steps down the pitch, in preparation to taking a run, just as the bowler bowls the ball.
For a fielder to position himself behind the wicket (relative to a team-mate who is throwing the ball at the wicket) so as to stop the ball, and prevent overthrows.
(of a blockage) To halt the flow or movement of something.
definitions.example: When I flushed the toilet, the plumbing backed up and burst.

definitions.definition: To fill up because of a backlog.
A situation which poses an immediate risk and which requires urgent attention.
definitions.example: Cardiac arrest is an emergency and if you find someone in cardiac arrest you should call 999 immediately.

definitions.definition: The department of a hospital that treats emergencies.
An individual brought in at short notice to replace a member of staff, a player in a sporting team, etc.
The quality of being emergent; sudden or unexpected appearance; an unforeseen occurrence.
To send out or give off
Fastidiously; carefully.
Precisely; with fine discernment or judgement.
Pleasantly; satisfactorily.
(notcomp) Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of stars.
Heavenly.
(by extension) Exceptional.
definitions.example: The actress gave a stellar performance.

definitions.definition: To participate, cooperate, or conform
definitions.example: I refuse to go along with another of his dangerous schemes.

definitions.definition: To move along or proceed
A lyric poem.
(also in plural) The words of a song or other vocal music. The singular form often refers to a part of the words, whereas the plural form can refer to all of the words.
definitions.example: The lyric in line 3 doesn't rhyme.

definitions.definition: The words to a song (or other vocal music).
A lyric poem.
(also in plural) The words of a song or other vocal music. The singular form often refers to a part of the words, whereas the plural form can refer to all of the words.
definitions.example: The lyric in line 3 doesn't rhyme.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to a type of poetry (such as a sonnet or ode) that expresses subjective thoughts and feelings, often in a songlike style
Of or relating to a writer of such poetry
Lyrical
Having a light singing voice of modest range
Of or relating to musical drama and opera
Melodious
Of or relating to the lyre (or sometimes the harp)
One of the components of a compound leaf.
A small plant leaf.
definitions.example: The new leaflets at the end of the branch were a lighter shade of green than the mature leaves.

definitions.definition: A small sheet of paper containing information, used for dissemination of said information, often an advertisement.
definitions.example: A leaflet had been left under the car's windshield wiper.

definitions.definition: A flap of a valve of a heart or blood vessel.
To distribute leaflets to.
definitions.example: A sidewalk preacher gave an impassioned sermon while an assistant leafleted those who stayed to listen.

definitions.definition: To distribute leaflets.
definitions.example: During the summer, Peter earned some extra cash by leafleting for a local pizza delivery restaurant.

definitions.definition: A hill or mountain.
Any of seven fleshy prominences in the palm of the hand, taken to represent the influences of various heavenly bodies.
definitions.example: the mount of Jupiter

definitions.definition: A bulwark for offence or defence; a mound.
A bank; a fund.
A green hillock in the base of a shield.
An everlasting flower.
A durable cloth fabric for shoes, etc.
Lasting or enduring forever; existing or continuing without end
Continuing indefinitely, or during a long period; perpetual; sometimes used, colloquially, as a strong intensive.
definitions.example: this everlasting nonsense

definitions.definition: Existing with infinite temporal duration (as opposed to existence outside of time).
Extremely.
A type of incense obtained from the Boswellia thurifera tree.
A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.
Any permanent army post.
An outlying trading-station, as in British North America.
To create a fort, fortifications, a strong point, or a redoubt.
An amount by which a quantity is increased.
For a quantity, the act or process of becoming larger
Offspring, progeny
The creation of one or more new stitches; see Increase (knitting).
(of a quantity, etc.) To become larger or greater.
definitions.example: His rage only increased when I told him of the lost money.

definitions.definition: To make (a quantity, etc.) larger.
To multiply by the production of young; to be fertile, fruitful, or prolific.
To become more nearly full; to show more of the surface; to wax.
definitions.example: The Moon increases.

definitions.definition: An item of casual clothing: a top with no sleeves.
The top of a ship's bilge tank, usually the lowest horizontal surface on board.
Any of several species of small, mostly European rodents of the family Gliridae; also called Myoxidae or Muscardinidae by some taxonomists.
Glis glis, the edible dormouse
Muscardinus avellanarius, the hazel dormouse.
A person who sleeps a great deal, or who falls asleep readily (by analogy with the sound hibernation of the dormouse).
A bodyguard or unit of bodyguards, a guard of someone's (especially a king's) life or person.
An attendant, usually an expert swimmer, employed to save swimmers in trouble or near drowning at a body of water.
A lifesaver.
A sturdy metal bracket fixed in front of each of the leading wheels of a train to deflect small objects away from the wheels to prevent derailment.
(UK politics) Someone who supports Brexit, the United Kingdom's exit from the European Union.
One who leaves.
definitions.example: He's a leaver, not a stayer: he'll never be happy tied down in one place.

definitions.definition: To occur or take place.
definitions.example: Let me tell you how it happened.

definitions.definition: To happen to; to befall.
(with infinitive) To do or occur by chance or unexpectedly.
definitions.example: Do you happen to have an umbrella?

definitions.definition: (followed by on or upon) To encounter by chance.
Maybe, perhaps.
One who ranges; a rover.
A keeper, guardian, or soldier who ranges over a region (generally of wilderness) to protect the area or enforce the law.
That which separates or arranges; a sieve.
A dog that beats the ground in search of game.
In some modern armies, an elite soldier, similar to special forces but often operating in larger units.
A warrior character, often with wilderness and stealth skill, who typically travels the countryside.
A character skilled in the use of ranged weapons.
To work as a ranger.
An ungulate animal; a hooved mammal.
definitions.example: The majority of large land mammals are ungulates.

definitions.definition: Having hooves.
Shaped like a hoof.

The bone of the forearm that extends from the elbow to the wrist on the side opposite to the thumb, corresponding to the fibula of the hind limb. Also, the corresponding bone in the forelimb of any vertebrate.
